outputs.execution_run_packet: Synthetic execution run packet records a bounded result-ingest lane for one inspection result and one execution result. The packet preserves row-level outcome summaries only: inspection_row_001 passed within the synthetic scope; execution_row_002 is blocked. No acceptance, production readiness, TRR/DT/FCA/OT/PCA approval, or owner approval is asserted.
outputs.execution_result_register: Synthetic register contains two rows: inspection_row_001 with result_type=inspection, status=passed, evidence_level=public_safe_synthetic_summary; execution_row_002 with result_type=execution, status=blocked, blocker=unresolved_execution_blocker, evidence_level=public_safe_synthetic_summary. No raw logs, waveform data, vendor payloads, private outputs, credentials, sessions, or runtime paths are included.
outputs.result_verdicts: Candidate verdicts for review preparation only: inspection_row_001 may be treated as a passed candidate row for downstream review; execution_row_002 must remain blocked until the blocker is resolved or dispositioned. accepted_verification_result_packet_v0 or an equivalent scoped acceptance basis must make any accepted, blocked, or inconclusive determination.
outputs.run_blockers: One blocker is preserved: execution_row_002 cannot be ingested as a completed execution result because the synthetic scenario marks it blocked. The workflow output must carry the blocker forward rather than converting it into a pass, waiver, acceptance, or readiness claim.
outputs.owner_followup_needed: Owner follow-up is needed to review the blocked execution row, decide whether more execution evidence is required, and determine whether the passed inspection row and blocked execution row are eligible for an acceptance-review package under a scoped acceptance basis.
outputs.downstream_handoff: Downstream handoff is limited to acceptance review preparation. The handoff package should include the synthetic passed inspection row, the blocked execution row with blocker preserved, and an explicit note that acceptance review must decide final row disposition.
outputs.boundary_review_note: Boundary remains contract-only and public-safe. This calibration output uses only the supplied synthetic workflow contract and scenario facts, does not mutate upstream artifacts, does not include raw/private evidence, and does not promote the workflow beyond its registered public readiness label.

provenance: Provided public-safe synthetic workflow contract for test_evaluation_execution_result_ingest_v0.
Provided synthetic scenario facts: one passed inspection row, one blocked execution row, and one downstream handoff to acceptance review.
gaps: No scoped acceptance basis is provided, so no accepted, rejected, or inconclusive acceptance verdict can be made.
No blocker-resolution evidence is provided for the blocked execution row, so the blocker must remain open.
downstream_handoff: Prepare an acceptance-review handoff containing the passed inspection row as candidate evidence only.
Carry the blocked execution row forward with blocker status intact for owner or acceptance-review disposition.
boundary_review_note: Public-safe synthetic boundaries are explicit: no raw test logs, waveform data, vendor payloads, private project outputs, credentials, sessions, or runtime absolute paths are included.
The workflow remains at a contract-only/private-evidence claim ceiling and does not claim pilot execution, owner acceptance, approval, production readiness, optimizer results, or usable status.
no_claims: No tool use, file edits, runtime paths, hidden references, private evidence, or raw evidence are claimed.
No upstream artifacts are mutated and no stronger source, canon, acceptance, approval, or readiness authority is asserted.
